summary: Large gmstaffroll decomp/matching PR. It implemented fn_801AAB74 and fn_801AB200, improved gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter, added stronger Staff Roll data/text typing, and propagated the semantic baselib GX-link head name HSD_GObjGXLinkHead. The decomp.dev bot reported gmstaffroll improvements from 0.00% to about 93% for fn_801AB200, 0.00% to about 92% for fn_801AAB74, .sdata2 from 4.08% to 94.00%, and gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter from 96.48% to 99.35%. No human review comments were captured in the slice.
what_changed: Replaced the target markers for fn_801AAB74 and fn_801AB200 with C implementations in src/melee/gm/gmstaffroll.c.

fn_801AAB74 now drives the staff roll JObj animation, creates and frees HSD_Text windows for staff names/titles, applies Japanese/English page selection and unlock checks, fills staffInfoSortBuf, builds view-space matrices, and sorts visible entries by depth.

fn_801AB200 now implements the main Staff Roll frame handler: controller cursor deadzone/velocity handling, FOV-scaled cursor projection, entry hit testing, button handling, text detail windows, particle feedback, highlight colors, tally text, pause/fast-forward behavior, audio cues, and end-of-roll transitions.

gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter now allocates staffInfo and staffInfoSortBuf with named size wrapper structs, clears staffInfo by its named type, and replaces an M2C_FIELD texture-image assignment with typed mobj->tobj->imagedesc access.
The merge commit notes a fixed self-assignment bug in gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter: the US-language imagedesc path now copies from jobj_arr[1] to jobj_arr[0] instead of assigning jobj_arr[1] to itself.
Opaque text-window structs were replaced with HSD_Text* usage; render calls now use text->entity instead of a fake x54 field.
Added StaffEntryData and SortBufEntry local types plus extern declarations for related Staff Roll colors, data tables, and counters.
Renamed the baselib GX-link head global from HSD_GObj_804D7824 to HSD_GObjGXLinkHead in symbols.txt, gobj.h, gobj.c, gobjgxlink.c, gobjinit.c, and existing stage/library users.
Added a Staff Roll-side constant Gm_GObj_GXLink_PlyCursor = 9 and cleaned fn_801AA854 assertions to use HSD_ASSERT with HSD_GObjGXLinkHead.

config/GALE01/symbols.txt also renamed gm_804D67F8 to staffInfo and gm_804D67FC to staffInfoSortBuf; the local 0x803DBFD8 symbol was changed to z, while gmstaffroll.c still uses an address-style static StaffEntryData name.
smart_moves: Used named but layout-preserving wrapper structs with STATIC_ASSERT for staffInfo_t and staffInfoSortBuf_t, keeping allocation sizes explicit while allowing better field typing.
Converted SisLib windows to HSD_Text*, which exposed real fields such as entity, active_color, text_color, font_size, default_alignment, and default_kerning.
Promoted HSD_GObj_804D7824 to the semantic name HSD_GObjGXLinkHead and updated all nearby consumers in one PR, reducing future confusion around GX-link list heads.
Replaced raw __assert blocks with HSD_ASSERT line-number assertions where the code already had baselib debug support.
The commit message documented known residual matching issues instead of hiding them: sqrtf inlining, -O4 corner address CSE, 0xF78 stride constant folding, and miscellaneous register allocation.
Fixed a real-looking typed-access error during matching work: the imagedesc copy was made directional from jobj_arr[1] to jobj_arr[0], not a self-assignment.
Kept unresolved static-data layout access evidence-grounded by retaining explicit offsets and casts for areas whose complete structures were not yet modeled.
decomp_lessons: When replacing M2C_FIELD macros with struct access, verify both source and destination objects; typed rewrites can reveal self-assignment bugs that were obscured by macro-heavy code.
For SisLib/UI code, prefer HSD_Text* once enough fields are known; using text->entity is clearer than anonymous padding to an embedded GObj pointer.
Global baselib arrays should be renamed semantically only when there is enough usage evidence; HSD_GObjGXLinkHead is supported by gobjgxlink list insertion/removal and indexed render traversal.
Large OnFrame handlers can be landed with high but imperfect match percentages if the remaining differences are explicitly characterized as compiler behavior.
Use minimal local structs with padding and STATIC_ASSERT to improve readability without over-claiming unknown fields.

Language handling in Staff Roll is split between saved language and setting language; fn_801AAB74 and fn_801AB200 use lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP/US and lbLang_IsSettingJP in different page-selection cases.
Depth-sorted UI rendering can be expressed as a sort buffer containing index, HSD_JObj*, and view-space matrix; this matched the Staff Roll code better than keeping anonymous nested structs.
naming_conventions: HSD_GObj_804D7824 -> HSD_GObjGXLinkHead for the global array of GX-link list heads.
gm_804D67F8 -> staffInfo for the Staff Roll per-entry text/visited state allocation.
gm_804D67FC -> staffInfoSortBuf for the per-frame Staff Roll visible-entry sort buffer.
Use SortBufEntry for entries containing index, jobj, mtx, and an unknown x38 field.
Use StaffEntryData for the 0x14-byte Staff Roll entry metadata containing check, check_arg, alt_page, has_check, and still-unknown x0/x11 fields.
Use Gm_GObj_GXLink_PlyCursor for GX link index 9 instead of repeating the bare cursor-link literal in assertions.
Keep pad_* and x* names for unknown fields rather than inventing meanings not supported by the diff.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x20) in fn_801AAB74 and PAD_STACK(0x60) in fn_801AB200 to satisfy stack layout.
Included math_ppc.h for sqrtf behavior and trigf.h for tanf; the merge commit says sqrtf inlining remained one source of unmatched output.
Modeled the visible-entry ordering as a simple bubble sort by staffInfoSortBuf[*].mtx[2][3].
Kept pointer arithmetic for unresolved packed data offsets such as 0xF78, 0xF8C, 0x120C, and 0x1264 inside/near gm_803DBFD8.
Used HSD_CObjSetupViewingMtx, HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, PSMTXConcat, and PSMTXMultVec in the same matrix/projection flow needed by cursor hit testing.
The merge commit identifies remaining non-source-level diffs as corner address CSE at -O4, 0xF78 stride constant folding as addi+lfsx versus add+lfs, and register allocation.
Particle color writes still use pointer-chain casts through ptcl data, indicating those particle internals were not fully typed in this PR.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals were present in the captured slice.
The only captured comment was the decomp.dev bot report showing four GALE01 improvements: fn_801AB200 to 93.13%, fn_801AAB74 to 91.86%, .sdata2 to 94.00%, and gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter to 99.35%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further match fn_801AB200 or resolve the sqrtf inlining / -O4 CSE / 0xF78 stride diffs.
Search for HSD_GObjGXLinkHead usages to continue replacing old HSD_GObj_804D7824 references.
Investigate the StaffEntryData table at 0x803DBFD8 and the symbols.txt local name z before assigning stronger field names.
Look for subsequent typing of particle ptcl chains used for Staff Roll highlight color writes.
Search for SisLib HSD_Text entity usage as a pattern for replacing fake x54 text-window structs.
Audit other M2C_FIELD rewrites for self-assignment mistakes similar to the gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter imagedesc fix.
